published_prior_art.cite: Puterman1994 and Altman1999: standard finite-state average-reward/occupation-measure support formulas

repo_prior_art.cite: ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study is static bounded-response; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2/v3 are two-stage IV memory bounds; pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 is finite DID support-function anticipation; none has reversible-spell Cesaro limits

published_prior_art.cite: Han2021 finite dynamic sequence effects; ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013 and JunLeeShin2016 panel bounds; Schneider1993/RockafellarWets1998 cover generic Hausdorff/support/argmax stability
none among verified dynamic-treatment, finite-bounds, MDP, and panel partial-ID papers

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The bibkey/year for DeChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2026 is unstable: DOI 10.1162/rest_a_01414 is listed by MIT Press as Review of Economics and Statistics, February 9, 2024, pages 1-45, not 2026 pages 1-18.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The stationary reward support formula is already standard Markov reward/occupation-measure algebra in Puterman1994 and Altman1999; keep it as background, not a novelty-bearing theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest mathematical prior art is missing: support-function/Hausdorff convergence and argmax stability from convex and variational analysis are more direct than the econometric comparators for the face diagnostic.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; to reach flagship, the kernel needs a causal sharp-bound object that is not just a generic compact-set face-stability criterion, or a named wider-class extension left open by prior literature.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Endpoint interval convergence does not imply Hausdorff-Cauchy convergence of the full embedded polytopes or faces; objective-null coordinates can oscillate while lower/upper support values converge.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The text calls \mathfrak D_T computable/observable, but \mathfrak D_T(\varepsilon)=\sup_{J\ge1}\mathfrak D_{T,J} depends on all future windows; only \mathfrak D_{T,J} is finite-window observable.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The two-face construction still lacks a concrete mechanism forcing infinitely many endpoint switches; opposite signs of a decaying boundary correction on two affine spans do not by themselves produce subsequence cycling.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Conjecture 1's iff with either full polytope/face convergence as the left-hand condition or a weaker endpoint-only diagnostic based on the two support values in directions \pm L.
Add convex-analysis prior art and state the genuinely causal delta: which part is not already support-function continuity, Berge maximum theorem, or Hausdorff convergence of compact convex sets.
For Conjecture 2, give a two-state numerical witness template with explicit rewards, initial distribution, and finite-horizon projected sets for at least two horizon parities.

Correct the de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille bibliography year/pages and keep the comparison as a 2024 Review of Economics and Statistics online article unless a final 2026 issue assignment is documented.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1 to remove the false endpoint-to-full-face equivalence, add the missing convex-analysis comparison, and decide whether the flagship claim is a causal sharp-bound theorem rather than a generic stability criterion.
